shot.subject: water
shot.notes: the water surface from above, sky reflected, nothing visible below

segments.text: To every animal we have met so far, this is where the world ends. The ants turn back here. The ladybugs drown here. Even the birds only read the surface, like a page they cannot turn.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: the camera slides beneath the surface; light shafts angle down

segments.text: But cross the mirror, and the world does not end at all. It starts over.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: a fish swims through frame, calm and certain, scales catching the light

segments.text: Welcome to the drowned garden. These are the fish.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: swooping run over the flooded terrain, hills and hollows below

segments.text: The floor of this country is the same land the Gardener sculpted — the same hills, the same hollows — simply claimed by water instead of air. Where the ground dips below the water table, a lake exists. Where it rises, a shore. The fish live inside the shape of the land, like air poured into a mold.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: macro on the tail's rhythm, the body's easy S-curve

segments.text: And they fit it beautifully. No legs to carry, no weight to fight. A fish moves by conviction — one traveling wave, nose to tail, and the water does the rest.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: circle a cruising fish; others pass at different depths behind it

segments.text: They hold their depth the way birds hold altitude, layered through the water column from the sunlit ceiling to the dim floor. Same instinct, different fluid. The garden, it turns out, invented flight twice.

shot.subject: water
shot.notes: from above: rings ripple where food pellets dot the surface

segments.text: Meals here arrive from the sky. Whatever falls onto the mirror — drifting motes, sinking crumbs, the garden's slow fallout — becomes the property of whoever reaches it first.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: a fish tilts upward and rises toward a sinking pellet

segments.text: A sinking morsel is a public auction, and everyone bids. The fish patrol beneath the ceiling, reading it for silhouettes and ripples with the same attention the birds give the ground. Two worlds, mirrored at the surface, both staring at the same boundary from opposite sides.

shot.subject: ladybug
shot.notes: a ladybug struggles at the flooded margin, seen from below

segments.text: And sometimes the boundary blunders in. When the waters rise over low ground, the drowned meadow briefly serves land-food — a ladybug where a ladybug should never be. The fish are not sentimental about windfalls. In the shallows, geography is the best hunter of all.

shot.subject: shore
shot.notes: the waterline itself: water lapping at grass, advancing and retreating

segments.text: Because here is the strangest thing about the drowned country: its borders move. Twice in every lunar day, the moon leans on this water and the whole nation swells — up the beach, into the grass, over the low places.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: glide through newly flooded ground, grass blades standing in water

segments.text: At high tide, the fish swim through what was lately a meadow — a drowned forest of grass, briefly theirs, on loan from the moon.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: a fish turns back from thinning shallows as the water retreats

segments.text: Then the tide lets go, the border retreats, and the nation shrinks to its old shape. The fish give ground without complaint. Their whole world breathes, and they have simply learned to live inside the lungs.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: a dozen fish moving loosely together, spacing even, headings aligned

segments.text: Watch a group cruise the middle water, and the birds' episode replays itself in a heavier fluid. The same three unwritten rules — stay close, don't collide, follow — produce the same conjuring trick: many bodies, one motion. The garden invented the flock twice, and signed neither copy.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: the school jinks as one; a beat later, the stragglers snap into the new heading

segments.text: Startle them, and the trick shows its seams beautifully. The turn ripples through the school like a whipcrack — leaders first, laggards a heartbeat behind — information traveling body to body faster than any fish can see. No alarm was sounded. The school simply is its own alarm.

shot.subject: fish
shot.notes: two different fish side by side: different sizes, different flank colors

segments.text: And like the ants and the butterflies, the drowned nation comes in varieties. The Gardener stocked these waters with more than one lineage — different sizes, different liveries, sharing the water column the way the blade types share the meadow. Ancestry written on the flank, in colors only the sunlit shallows ever properly display.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: the school passes overhead in silhouette against the bright ceiling

segments.text: From below, variety vanishes and only the pattern remains — a drifting constellation of dark hulls against the silver roof. It is the drowned country's version of the birds crossing the sky, and if the two nations could ever compare notes, they would recognize each other instantly.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: from the lake floor, looking up: the bright, trembling ceiling

segments.text: Spend long enough down here, and the surface stops looking like an exit and starts looking like a sky — silver in the day, black at night, streaked and hammered when the rain comes down.

shot.subject: rain
shot.notes: from below: raindrops strike the ceiling in bursts of light

segments.text: A storm, from underneath, is a drumroll played on the roof of the world. The fish barely stir.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: the whole water column at dusk: fish layered in fading light

segments.text: Above that ceiling live giants, lanterns, and toll collectors they will never meet. The fish are no poorer for it. Theirs is the calmest country in the garden — cool, quiet, and complete.

shot.subject: underwater
shot.notes: the camera pushes into deeper, darker water; the floor falls away

segments.text: Or almost complete. Because in the deepest reach of this drowned country, something moves that makes every fish in frame look like a minnow. Something the size of geography.

shot.subject: whale
shot.notes: from the gloom, an enormous eye slides past the lens

segments.text: The deep keeps one secret in this garden, and it is next. The whale.
